required_edits: Set atropos/memory/CONTRACTS/supersession.json status to RATIFICATION-PENDING (not NORMATIVE-CURRENT) until this council's authorization completes and the authority triple {plan sha256, authz-N, decision-atropos-cycle-1 id} attaches, exactly as §6 of the plan specifies.

Align becomes_normative_when in the contract with §6: normative activation requires ratification + comprehension gate GRANTED + verify-contracts integration (the future-atropos-unimplemented -> implemented flip), not merely 'test-verify.mjs and test-readonly.mjs pass (met)'.

Add the authority triple fields (authz reference and decision-atropos-cycle-1 content address) to the contract's authority block as pending placeholders or attach them post-ratification per the plan's own sequence — the contract currently cites only pre_review and design_ruling while claiming NORMATIVE-CURRENT.
hard_stops: The submitted contract self-declares status: NORMATIVE-CURRENT before this council has ratified, directly contradicting the plan's own §6 which mandates RATIFICATION-PENDING until authorization and conditions normativity on ratification + comprehension gate + verify-contracts integration. Authorizing a packet whose normative artifact already asserts the authority this council is being asked to grant would ratify a self-contradiction and pre-empt the deferred-ratification sequence the plan itself defines.
rationale: The plan itself is strong and I would sign it: the scope is honest (verification half of 'retires', read-only, plan-version surface only), the NON-CLAIMs are explicit and correctly bounded (no node-backed verification, no completeness-of-universe proof, no cryptographic authentication of CURRENT-AUTHORITY, read-only oracle is evidence not proof), the fail-closed ingestion/verifier split is coherent (file-level anomalies throw; per-entry malformation yields a verdict, never a silent skip), the discovery is manifest-anchored and realpath-contained at every level, the terminal authority is disk-resolved rather than syntactic, the multi-hop chain fixture discriminates transitive resolution, and the boundary/trust posture (zero-dependency, no clotho import, sole sanctioned vendor import) matches the repository trust model. The build-before-council sequence is disclosed honestly under The Eye's recorded build-mode directive and framed as a non-precedent — acceptable for a deferred ratification. The single blocking defect is internal inconsistency between the plan and the contract submitted alongside it: §6 states the contract is RATIFICATION-PENDING until this council authorizes and that NORMATIVE-CURRENT may be claimed ONLY after ratification plus the comprehension gate plus verify-contracts integration, yet the contract as submitted already declares status NORMATIVE-CURRENT with becomes_normative_when marked '(met)' on tests alone. The objective's own bar is 'no remaining contradiction'; this is one, and it is exactly the kind of authority-sequencing claim a supersession verifier's own memory must get right. The fix is mechanical (three field edits), after which I would approve as-is.
